campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help fund youth recreation scholarships. They also discussed extending a temporary local rule and moving forward with plans to build a bicycle bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, including applying for grant money to help pay for it.
keyTopics: Water Tower Kitchen donates $4,000 for youth sports scholarships
Extending temporary ordinance #2326 (details not fully specified)
Planning a new bicycle/pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
Applying for state grant funding for the Hamilton Avenue bridge project

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026, where residents could share their views on a proposed new rule or policy. After hearing from the public, the council moved to advance the proposal by skipping the usual first reading process and putting it directly on the fast-track agenda for final approval at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather community input
New ordinance (city rule) being considered
Council expedited the approval process by waiving first reading
Final vote expected at next regular council meeting

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 1, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a procedural meeting to organize their upcoming agendas for April 7th and April 14th, 2026. They reviewed what items should be added to each meeting's schedule and determined if any topics needed special time slots or legal notices.
keyTopics: Finalized the April 7, 2026 council meeting agenda
Reviewed draft items for the April 14, 2026 meeting
Assigned time slots to specific agenda items as needed
Processed public records from March 19-26, 2026

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a routine meeting on March 30, 2026 where they approved previous meeting minutes and opened the floor for residents to speak about issues on their minds. The council also reviewed reports from various committees and boards, and discussed upcoming meeting schedules for different city commissions.
keyTopics: Public comment period for residents to raise concerns
Approval of previous meeting minutes
Committee and staff reports
Upcoming board and commission meeting schedules

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal grant money that helps fund affordable housing and community services in Sunnyvale. The commission approved meeting minutes from the previous month and heard presentations from organizations seeking funding to support local housing and human services programs.
keyTopics: Federal housing grant applications being reviewed
Affordable housing and community services funding decisions
February meeting minutes approved
Local organizations presenting funding requests

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council focused on helping local businesses thrive by rolling out several support programs. They discussed a business license amnesty initiative to encourage previously unregistered businesses to get legal, launched outreach efforts to shopping centers, and planned summer promotional campaigns to encourage residents to shop locally. The city is also exploring partnerships with private companies to sponsor city events and offering financial incentives to attract new retail stores, small businesses, and startups to Cupertino.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - helping unregistered businesses become compliant
Outreach to shopping centers and local retailers
Summer of Celebrations - shop local promotional campaigns
Public-private partnerships for city event sponsorships
Economic incentives to attract retail, small businesses, and startups

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 2, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara held a Historical and Landmarks Commission hearing on April 2, 2026, where the city reviewed historical buildings or sites in the community. Residents could attend in person or join online via Zoom to watch and participate in the discussion.
keyTopics: Historical and Landmarks Commission hearing
Hybrid meeting format (in-person and Zoom)
Public comment deadline: 12 p.m. day of meeting via email
Review of historical properties or landmark designations in Santa Clara

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — March 17, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a routine meeting on March 17, 2026, to handle administrative tasks and approve upcoming plans. The council reviewed meeting schedules, approved previous meeting minutes, confirmed who will represent the city on the school district's economic committee, and authorized Mayor Montano to attend a wellness conference.
keyTopics: Approved previous meeting minutes and confirmed upcoming meeting calendar
Mayor Montano authorized to attend WELL 2026 Annual conference
Confirmed school district representative appointment for economic matters
Reviewed councilmembers' requested agenda items for future meetings

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026 to handle several routine matters. They recognized March as Women's History Month, approved an agreement with the American Red Cross to use city facilities during disasters, and authorized a $93,383 annual contract for email security software services for the city.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
Red Cross disaster relief facility agreement
Email security software contract ($93,383/year for 3 years)
